label: law-sharp
classification_reason: The witness exploits an object-definition bug on a boundary support face: in the D=d1 stratum, consistency gives Y1(d1)=Y1=1 and the diagonal input alpha_11=0 forces Y1(d0)=1, so the algebraic endpoint alpha_21=1 would require E[Y1(d2)|D=d1]=2 outside the bounded support. The LP geometry and critical-L mechanism survive once the ATT array is required to be mean-completable.
witness_property: The counterexample uses a bounded on-arm outcome at the upper support with zero diagonal effect, forcing the baseline potential outcome mean to the same upper boundary; the omitted condition is underline_y <= E[Y1|D=d_h] - tau_h(P) + alpha_jh <= overline_y for every coordinate.

mend.description: Replace the loose algebraic ATT-array polytope A_L(P) by the mean-completion polytope A_L^mc(P) = {alpha in A_L(P): underline_y <= E_P[Y1|D=d_h] - tau_h(P) + alpha_jh <= overline_y for all j,h}, and define the ACRT endpoints by optimizing over A_L^mc(P).

vacuity.witness_member: In the original three-dose law, the finite completion with rows D=d0:(Y1(d0),Y1(d1),Y1(d2))=(0,0,1), D=d1:(1,1,1), D=d2:(0,0,1) induces alpha=((0,0,0),(0,0,0),(1,0,1)) in A_1^mc(P).

substance.motivating_regime_retained: The mended class still contains finite ordered continuous-treatment DiD grids with CGS diagonal inputs, bounded potential outcomes, and row-wise Lipschitz or isotonic cross-dose ATT restrictions; it only removes arrays whose implied potential-outcome coordinate means leave the maintained support.

naturalness.reason: The correction is the standard sharp-identified-set convention of taking parameter values generated by latent random variables consistent with the observed data and maintained assumptions. Here it is the finite-dimensional projection of bounded potential-outcome support, not a witness-tailored exclusion.

lineage_note: Original law-sharp algebraic envelope refuted by the three-dose binary witness whose alpha_21=1 implies E[Y1(d2)|D=d1]=2; mended to law-sharpness of the mean-completion polytope via the coordinate bounded-mean object correction; H justified by Molinari2020, arXiv:2004.11751 / DOI 10.48550/arXiv.2004.11751.
mended_headline_statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:grid-bounds}--\ref{ass:completion}, with the algebraic object corrected to A_L^mc(P) = {alpha in A_L(P): underline_y <= E_P[Y1|D=d_h] - tau_h(P) + alpha_jh <= overline_y for all j,h}, the finite-grid ACRT sharp set equals [L_h^mc(L;P), U_h^mc(L;P)], the image of A_L^mc(P) under theta_h. Necessity follows because every bounded latent law has these coordinate means; sufficiency follows by a cellwise finite-support construction of bounded potential outcomes with the prescribed means.
